title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models

title: Instant Neural Graphics Primitives with a Multiresolution Hash Encoding

title: 3D Gaussian Splatting for Real-Time Radiance Field Rendering

title: Visual Instruction Tuning

title: Exploring the Limits of Transfer Learning with a Unified Text-to-Text Transformer
evidence: GitHub repository google-research/text-to-text-transfer-transformer is described as code for the paper "Exploring the Limits of Transfer Learning with a Unified Text-to-Text Transformer" and cites arXiv:1910.10683. Source: https://github.com/google-research/text-to-text-transfer-transformer

title: Diffusion Models Beat GANs on Image Synthesis
evidence: The arXiv paper page states that the authors release code at https://github.com/openai/guided-diffusion, and the OpenAI repo is the guided-diffusion codebase. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2105.05233
note: The arXiv paper page states that the authors release code at https://github.com/openai/guided-diffusion, and the OpenAI repo is the guided-diffusion codebase. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2105.05233

title: Towards Robust Monocular Depth Estimation: Mixing Datasets for Zero-shot Cross-dataset Transfer
evidence: The isl-org/MiDaS repository describes itself as code for robust monocular depth estimation from the Ranftl et al. paper "Towards Robust Monocular Depth Estimation: Mixing Datasets for Zero-shot Cross-dataset Transfer". Source: https://github.com/isl-org/MiDaS
note: The isl-org/MiDaS repository describes itself as code for robust monocular depth estimation from the Ranftl et al. paper "Towards Robust Monocular Depth Estimation: Mixing Datasets for Zero-shot Cross-dataset Transfer". Source: https://github.com/isl-org/MiDaS

title: wav2vec 2.0: A Framework for Self-Supervised Learning of Speech Representations
evidence: The fairseq wav2vec example documents wav2vec 2.0 as learning speech representations as described in "wav2vec 2.0: A Framework for Self-Supervised Learning of Speech Representations" and provides models/instructions under the fairseq repo. Source: https://github.com/facebookresearch/fairseq/tree/main/examples/wav2vec
note: The fairseq wav2vec example documents wav2vec 2.0 as learning speech representations as described in "wav2vec 2.0: A Framework for Self-Supervised Learning of Speech Representations" and provides models/instructions under the fairseq repo. Source: https://github.com/facebookresearch/fairseq/tree/main/examples/wav2vec

title: HuBERT: Self-Supervised Speech Representation Learning by Masked Prediction of Hidden Units

title: Structure-from-Motion Revisited
evidence: The COLMAP project repository is the source for COLMAP, the structure-from-motion and multi-view stereo software associated with the "Structure-from-Motion Revisited" publication. Source: https://github.com/colmap/colmap
note: The COLMAP project repository is the source for COLMAP, the structure-from-motion and multi-view stereo software associated with the "Structure-from-Motion Revisited" publication. Source: https://github.com/colmap/colmap

title: LightGCN: Simplifying and Powering Graph Convolution Network for Recommendation
evidence: The gusye1234/LightGCN-PyTorch repository describes itself as the official implementation of SIGIR 2020 paper "LightGCN: Simplifying and Powering Graph Convolution Network for Recommendation". Source: https://github.com/gusye1234/LightGCN-PyTorch
note: The gusye1234/LightGCN-PyTorch repository describes itself as the official implementation of SIGIR 2020 paper "LightGCN: Simplifying and Powering Graph Convolution Network for Recommendation". Source: https://github.com/gusye1234/LightGCN-PyTorch

title: YOLOv7: Trainable bag-of-freebies sets new state-of-the-art for real-time object detectors
evidence: The WongKinYiu/yolov7 repository describes itself as the implementation of paper "YOLOv7: Trainable bag-of-freebies sets new state-of-the-art for real-time object detectors" and is the benchmark expected official repository. Source: https://github.com/WongKinYiu/yolov7
note: Official identity bridge for YOLOv7 because live GitHub search can omit the exact WongKinYiu/yolov7 repo even though direct fetch succeeds.

title: YOLOv8
evidence: Ultralytics documentation and the ultralytics/ultralytics repository identify this package as the official Ultralytics YOLO implementation covering YOLOv8 models and workflows. Source: https://github.com/ultralytics/ultralytics
note: Ultralytics documentation and the ultralytics/ultralytics repository identify this package as the official Ultralytics YOLO implementation covering YOLOv8 models and workflows. Source: https://github.com/ultralytics/ultralytics

title: Deep Speech 2: End-to-End Speech Recognition in English and Mandarin
evidence: External paper-code references for "Deep Speech 2: End-to-End Speech Recognition in English and Mandarin" point to the PaddlePaddle/DeepSpeech repository; GitHub now returns a moved-repository response for that path. Source: https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/DeepSpeech
note: External paper-code references for "Deep Speech 2: End-to-End Speech Recognition in English and Mandarin" point to the PaddlePaddle/DeepSpeech repository; GitHub now returns a moved-repository response for that path. Source: https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/DeepSpeech

title: Collaborative Filtering for Implicit Feedback Datasets
evidence: The benfred/implicit repository implements implicit-feedback recommendation algorithms and is the benchmark high-quality reproduction/domain-library target for "Collaborative Filtering for Implicit Feedback Datasets". Source: https://github.com/benfred/implicit
note: Non-official reproduction/library identity: this mapping targets a mature implicit-feedback recommendation library, not an official paper-author repository.

title: DeepFM: A Factorization-Machine based Neural Network for CTR Prediction
evidence: The shenweichen/DeepCTR repository is a deep-learning CTR prediction package that includes DeepFM / CTR model implementations and is the benchmark high-quality reproduction/domain-library target. Source: https://github.com/shenweichen/DeepCTR
note: Non-official reproduction/library identity: this mapping targets a domain library implementation, not an official paper-author repository.
